data.title: Tesla stock rises, Robotaxi app opens to public, ride-hailing service expanding soon. Bulls eye aggressive entry.
data.description: Tesla's stock is on the rise, approaching a key entry point. The Tesla Robotaxi app is set to be launched to the public, offering a ride-hailing service that will soon be more widely available. Investors are showing optimism as they anticipate the potential growth in this venture.

data.title: Great Elm Capital Corp. (GECC) to launch public offering of unsecured notes due 2030, listed on Nasdaq within 30 days.
data.description: Great Elm Capital Corp (GECC) based in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida, revealed on September 4, 2025, the launch of an underwritten public offering of unsecured notes set to mature in 2030. The company, listed on the NASDAQ under ticker symbol GECC, anticipates these notes, named “Notes,” to be listed on The Nasdaq Global Market as “GECCG” and begin trading within 30 days from the issue date. The specifics regarding the interest rate and additional terms of the Notes will be settled through discussions between the Company and the underwriters.

data.title: Large Ethereum holders amassing ETH indicate potential price surge, backed by optimism from experts on ETF and treasury investments.
data.description: Analysts forecast a positive future for Ethereum as a result of growing whale holdings, positive sentiment towards ETFs, and increasing treasury investments.

data.title: DYDX price dips to $0.58, down 0.56% in 24 hours despite platform upgrades, testing critical support levels. Mixed signals from technical indicators noted amid latest community news.
data.description: The cryptocurrency DYDX is currently trading at $0.58, experiencing a decrease of 0.56% over the past 24 hours. This decline has led the currency to test crucial support levels amidst conflicting signals from technical indicators. Recent updates within the community have also influenced the situation.

data.title: Crypto prices stable before labor market data. Bitcoin $111,018.08, Ethereum $4,428.24, XRP $2.84, Dogecoin $0.2163. 93K traders liquidated for $225.01M in 24h. $301.3M inflows into Bitcoin ETFs, $38.2M outflows in Ethereum ETFs. Trader CrediBULL waits for $108,400 dip or $117,000 surge. Full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Cryptocurrency markets are experiencing a period of stability prior to the release of crucial labor market data later in the week. The current prices are as follows: Bitcoin (BTC) is trading at $111,018.08, Ethereum (ETH) at $4,428.24, Solana (SOL) at $208.79, XRP at $2.84, Dogecoin (DOGE) at $0.2163, and Shiba Inu (SHIB) at $0.00001223.Recent data reveals that 93,397 traders were liquidated in the last 24 hours, resulting in losses totaling $225.01 million. Furthermore, there were net inflows of $301.3 million into spot Bitcoin ETFs, while spot Ethereum ETFs saw net outflows of $38.2 million.CrediBULL Crypto stated he will only pay attention if the price drops below $108,400 or surpasses $117,000 with a strong upward movement, expressing frustration with the current market conditions. Michael van de Poppe also provided a warning. Want to read more? Find the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Carvana now offers same-day delivery in Seattle for select local customers buying or selling used cars.
data.description: Carvana, a leading company in the online marketplace for buying and selling used cars, has introduced same-day vehicle delivery in the wider Seattle region. This service is available to specific customers who place orders on Carvana.com, allowing them to receive their purchased vehicle on the very same day. Additionally, individuals in Seattle interested in selling their cars can benefit from immediate drop-off and pickup choices following the completion of Carvana's online appraisal process. This new offering highlights Carvana's commitment to providing efficient and convenient experiences for both buying and selling cars in the Seattle area.

data.title: Janitorial Manager in Toledo shares tips on hiring and keeping skilled cleaners amidst labor market competition.
data.description: In Toledo, Ohio, companies in the cleaning industry are adapting to overcome staffing shortages and high turnover rates. Janitorial Manager, led by President Archie Heinl, is advocating for five strategies to enhance recruitment and retention success. Emphasizing that a successful approach to attracting skilled cleaners involves more than just posting a job, Heinl highlights the importance of speed, transparency, and flexibility in the hiring process. By prioritizing these aspects, companies can better navigate the challenges posed by the current labor market dynamics.

data.title: Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Ltd. reports a 506% profit increase in H1 2025, reaching RMB 476 million, driven by green energy and computing sectors.
data.description: Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Ltd., listed as 00412.HK in Hong Kong, reported exceptional financial results for the first half of 2025. According to the H1 2025 Financial Report disclosed on August 29, the company witnessed a remarkable 506% increase in net profit, reaching RMB 476 million. This impressive performance was primarily attributed to the strategic shift towards renewable energy and computing power, which accounted for 96% of the total operating income of RMB 2.503 billion.

data.title: Wall Street speculating on Trump tariff refunds, with investors buying importers' rights in hopes of court reversal.
data.description: Investors are interested in purchasing importers’ claims to any refunds of the administration’s tariffs, hoping for a reversal of the tariffs by the courts. It is a risky bet with no guarantee of success.
